# Questions in Livestream 175

Live Stream #175: Meta the Meta, Meta Responses

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=562" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:22</a> | Was Solomon's Egyptian father-in-law more likely Siamun or Shoshenq II? | He leans toward Siamun. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=956" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:56</a> | Does the church's historical testimony have a lesser, relative authority under Scripture? | Yes. He keeps Scripture as final authority while still granting church councils and tradition real subordinate weight. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=1246" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:46</a> | Did the prophecy in Jeremiah 43:10-13 happen? | He discusses the prophecy but says he cannot provide the requested source during the stream. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=1695" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:15</a> | What do you think of translating the Exodus chariot term as "third man charioteers" based on Akkadian war texts? | He rejects the argument, noting surviving Egyptian chariots are two-person chariots and treating the proposed reading as dubious. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=2387" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:47</a> | What criteria should be used when ranking doctrines in theological triage? | He prioritizes doctrines tied to historic Christian essentials, especially resurrection, Trinity, salvation, the fall, and Christ's life. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=2887" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:07</a> | What do you think of Gavin Ortlund's criteria for theological triage? | He thinks the criteria are generally good, while noting the historical-church-testimony criterion can become more complicated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=3303" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:03</a> | Why does Elihu go unmentioned in God's rebuke in Job 42? | He does not treat that omission as proving Elihu was wholly right; the silence does not settle the issue by itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=3657" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:57</a> | Is the Bulletin for Biblical Research supplement series good scholarship? | Yes. He calls the Institute for Biblical Research a solid, more conservative scholarly group and notes he has published in the series. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=3780" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:00</a> | What do you think of Heiser's Old Testament hermeneutic that starts with ancient Near Eastern context? | He approves starting with the text's original context before moving to later christological readings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=4183" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:43</a> | What is the best commentary on Exodus? | He says the one he reaches for first is Nahum Sarna's JPS Torah commentary on Exodus. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=4400" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:20</a> | Which commentary gives plenty of information on Exodus 6:3? | He says he would need to check across commentaries and does not have a specific recommendation ready. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=4460" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:20</a> | Would you rather have an Exodus commentary that ignores Egyptian connections or one written by Scott Stripling? | He chooses the former, saying Stripling would not produce a good commentary on the topic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=4565" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:05</a> | Is there a commentary about commentaries? | Yes. He mentions older guidebooks to commentaries by D. A. Carson for the New Testament and Tremper Longman III for the Old Testament. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=4673" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:53</a> | Is the Transfiguration connected to Exodus 24 and 1 Kings 19? | He declines the question as too broad and asks for a narrower proposed connection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=5092" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:52</a> | Has *Ark of the Covenant* been used as course literature? | Yes. He says Carmen Imes used it in a Biola course. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=5535" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:15</a> | Did you ever interact with Gordon Fee? | No. He says they were never in the same place at the same time, similar to his missed opportunities to meet Heiser. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=5664" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:24</a> | Why does God not heal amputees? Is that an argument against God? | He says God can heal but does not always do so, and suffering can still be caught up into God's purposes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=5859" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:39</a> | Do Mark 10:11-12 and Matthew 19:8-9 contradict each other about divorce? | He reads them in context of Jesus' exchange with the Pharisees and does not treat them as a simple contradiction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=7076" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:56</a> | Should pastors raise up elders or deacons first? | He says leaders should raise people organically and watch whether they prove fit for elder or deacon work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=7228" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:28</a> | Why do people use Joshua in Zechariah to argue that Messiah will be a high priest? | He cannot locate the exact passage from the prompt and says the question needed a more specific reference. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=8838" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:27:18</a> | Could you explain what functional creation means? | He says he is not sure what the question means and cannot answer it clearly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=9734" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:14</a> | Do you need recommendations when applying for a funded PhD program? | Yes. He says PhD programs generally require recommendations, usually from former professors. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=9795" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:15</a> | Is Jude affirming the prophecy from Enoch in Jude 14-15 as historical? | He says Jude is clearly using 1 Enoch, but that does not settle every question about the text's canonical or historical status. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=10053" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:33</a> | Is the church still wealthy? | Not necessarily. He says some churches or individuals have wealth, but many churches also face people trying to exploit benevolence funds. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=10595" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:35</a> | What should Christian men look for in a future wife during dating? | He stresses being equally yoked in convictions, goals, and practical life direction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=11018" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:38</a> | Is marrying a robot wife acceptable for a Christian marriage? | No. He says a robot is not human and frames the idea as idolatrous. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=11331" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:51</a> | Was monism a thing in the ancient world? | Not really. He says pantheism is probably the closest ancient analogue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=11607" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:13:27</a> | Is the Virgin Mary the Mother of God and the Queen of Heaven? | He affirms Theotokos language for the incarnation while carefully distinguishing that from saying Mary is mother of the Trinity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=12205" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:25</a> | Did Egyptians have wedding anniversaries? | He says we do not know, and describes Egyptian marriage as mainly a civil arrangement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=13112" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:38:32</a> | Have you read any of Randy Alcorn's books, fiction or nonfiction? | No. He says he has not read Alcorn's books and cannot comment on the angelic-character material. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/bGY1zwr_wss?t=13215" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:40:15</a> | Can Psalm 82 be read as speaking to men because it says "you shall die like men"? | He says the comparison language does not force a human-only reading. |
